etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth; soil") + phonetic 矣(矣) (yǐ) -> dust; angstrom
etymology.interpretation: The character 埃(埃) (āi, "dust") combines 土(土) (tǔ, "earth; soil") to represent the idea of something related to the ground, with 矣(矣) (yǐ) functioning as a phonetic component to indicate pronunciation. The pairing suggests a small particle or angstrom, embodying the concept of dust as minute earth particles.
etymology.simplification: 埃 is the simplified and most commonly used form of the character, derived directly to function in modern contexts without further simplification.

examples.chinese: 一层薄薄的埃覆盖了家具(一層薄薄的埃覆蓋了傢俱)。
examples.pinyin: Yī céng báo báo de āi fùgài le jiājù.
examples.english: A thin layer of dust covered the furniture.

examples.chinese: 他用抹布擦掉书架上的埃(他用抹布擦掉書架上的埃)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng mābù cā diào shūjià shàng de āi.
examples.english: He wiped off the dust on the bookshelf with a cloth.

examples.chinese: 科学家使用埃作为长度单位来测量(科學家使用埃作為長度單位來測量)。
examples.pinyin: Kēxuéjiā shǐyòng āi zuòwéi chángdù dānwèi lái cèliáng.
examples.english: Scientists use angstrom as a unit of length for measurement.
